One Link
How it works Features Security Live mesh Share Builders About Get One Link
About

The internet was supposed to be ours.

Somewhere along the way it became a place where five companies hold every message, every photo, every file you ever made. One Link is the quiet way back.

What we are

One Link is a free, private network for messages, files, and devices. It works on phones, laptops, tablets, and small embedded boards. It works on a plane, on a boat, in a basement with no internet. It works when the relay we run goes dark, because anyone can run a relay.

Built so a single person, a family, a school, a movement, or a whole town can talk and share without anyone in the middle. Built so a journalist can hand documents to a source without leaving a trace. Built so a kid can send a 50GB game to their friend without uploading it to anyone first.

What we are not.

Not a company. Not a startup. Not a platform. Not a service. There is no pricing page coming. There is no enterprise tier coming. There is no acquisition story. We took an AGPL license on purpose. If anyone tries to fork One Link and lock it up, the license forces the source open again.

We do not have a sales team. We do not have a roadmap of features we have not built. We do not run analytics. We do not capture leads. If we shut down tomorrow, your One Link still works because it does not depend on us.

Who is behind it

Anyone willing to read the code, run a relay, file a bug, or argue with a design decision. There is no company. There are no founders to put on a slide. The work belongs to whoever picks it up. The license (AGPL) makes that promise enforceable.

The protocol grew out of a broader research effort on coherence-field topology, post-quantum cryptography, field-witness binding, and formal verification. One Link is the most public, most useful expression of that work. The lab and the math behind it are open too. We are one.

The covenant

We collect nothing. We sell nothing. We sign every release in a sealed environment and publish the proof. We will never add tracking. We will never add an account system. We will never lock features behind a tier. If we ever break any of these, the source license forces the world to fork around us.

That is the whole promise. Built into the architecture so we cannot break it even if we wanted to.

Get One Link → Donate
One Link

A free, private network for messages, files, and devices. Owned by no one. Belongs to everyone.

Built in the open. AGPL-3.0. No tracking, no analytics, no cookies. we are one
